correctness.notes: Theorem \ref{thm:joint-sharp-and-sep-bound}, full-class endpoint decomposition: The proof uses the missing condition Y(a)\in[y_L,y_U] Q-a.s. for every action a. Assumption \ref{ass:tail} only states support of the observed conditional law Y|A=a,X=x, not support of nonfactual potential outcomes.; Lemma \ref{lem:lp-envelope} consumed by Theorem \ref{thm:onestep-limit}: The one-step proof consumes a unique active LP threshold lambda_{eta,a}(x), but Assumptions \ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:nuisance} give no explicit uniqueness or canonical differentiable selection condition near eta_*; no-atom alone does not rule out threshold intervals in support gaps.; Definition of W_{tau,Gamma}^{band} and Theorem \ref{thm:band}: The pointwise envelopes \underline F_Gamma and \overline F_Gamma are not defined term-by-term. The proof requires, for example, \underline F_Gamma(y|P,pi)=E sum_a pi(a|X) inf_{ell_a<=r<=u_a, E[r|A=a,X]=1} E[r 1{Y<=y}|A=a,X], plus the corresponding upper envelope.; Minimal-assumption pass across headline theorems: The reproduced identification and band arguments do not consume iid sampling, and the band comparison does not consume consistency or the no-atom differentiability clause. The separated one-step limit law does not consume consistency or the MSM restriction except for causal interpretation.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem \ref{thm:joint-sharp-and-sep-bound}, full-class endpoint decomposition: The proof uses the missing condition Y(a)\in[y_L,y_U] Q-a.s. for every action a. Assumption \ref{ass:tail} only states support of the observed conditional law Y|A=a,X=x, not support of nonfactual potential outcomes. → Without potential-outcome support, the nonfactual term E_Q[(Y(a)-eta)_+|A!=a,X=x] is not bounded by (y_U-eta)_+ and can be made larger, so theta_U^{full} need not upper-bound sup Theta_I(P).

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Lemma \ref{lem:lp-envelope} consumed by Theorem \ref{thm:onestep-limit}: The one-step proof consumes a unique active LP threshold lambda_{eta,a}(x), but Assumptions \ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:nuisance} give no explicit uniqueness or canonical differentiable selection condition near eta_*; no-atom alone does not rule out threshold intervals in support gaps. → The derivative h_{eta,a}-T_{eta,a} and dot T_{eta,a,e} may be only a subgradient or selection-dependent, so the asymptotic linear expansion is not justified under the stated assumptions.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Definition of W_{tau,Gamma}^{band} and Theorem \ref{thm:band}: The pointwise envelopes \underline F_Gamma and \overline F_Gamma are not defined term-by-term. The proof requires, for example, \underline F_Gamma(y|P,pi)=E sum_a pi(a|X) inf_{ell_a<=r<=u_a, E[r|A=a,X]=1} E[r 1{Y<=y}|A=a,X], plus the corresponding upper envelope. → The band theorem is reproducible only under this intended interpretation; as written, the object \mathcal F_Gamma^{pt}(P,pi) is ambiguous and the inequality W_band>=underline W^{sep} is not a well-pinned mathematical claim.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Minimal-assumption pass across headline theorems: The reproduced identification and band arguments do not consume iid sampling, and the band comparison does not consume consistency or the no-atom differentiability clause. The separated one-step limit law does not consume consistency or the MSM restriction except for causal interpretation. → The stated theorem environments prove weaker-than-supported claims and obscure the actual frontier of assumptions needed for identification versus inference.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[blocking, setup] Theorem \ref{thm:joint-sharp-and-sep-bound}, full-class endpoint decomposition: The proof uses the missing condition Y(a)\in[y_L,y_U] Q-a.s. for every action a. Assumption \ref{ass:tail} only states support of the observed conditional law Y|A=a,X=x, not support of nonfactual potential outcomes.. Fix: Without potential-outcome support, the nonfactual term E_Q[(Y(a)-eta)_+|A!=a,X=x] is not bounded by (y_U-eta)_+ and can be made larger, so theta_U^{full} need not upper-bound sup Theta_I(P).
[serious, setup] Lemma \ref{lem:lp-envelope} consumed by Theorem \ref{thm:onestep-limit}: The one-step proof consumes a unique active LP threshold lambda_{eta,a}(x), but Assumptions \ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:nuisance} give no explicit uniqueness or canonical differentiable selection condition near eta_*; no-atom alone does not rule out threshold intervals in support gaps.. Fix: The derivative h_{eta,a}-T_{eta,a} and dot T_{eta,a,e} may be only a subgradient or selection-dependent, so the asymptotic linear expansion is not justified under the stated assumptions.
[serious, setup] Definition of W_{tau,Gamma}^{band} and Theorem \ref{thm:band}: The pointwise envelopes \underline F_Gamma and \overline F_Gamma are not defined term-by-term. The proof requires, for example, \underline F_Gamma(y|P,pi)=E sum_a pi(a|X) inf_{ell_a<=r<=u_a, E[r|A=a,X]=1} E[r 1{Y<=y}|A=a,X], plus the corresponding upper envelope.. Fix: The band theorem is reproducible only under this intended interpretation; as written, the object \mathcal F_Gamma^{pt}(P,pi) is ambiguous and the inequality W_band>=underline W^{sep} is not a well-pinned mathematical claim.
[serious, setup] Minimal-assumption pass across headline theorems: The reproduced identification and band arguments do not consume iid sampling, and the band comparison does not consume consistency or the no-atom differentiability clause. The separated one-step limit law does not consume consistency or the MSM restriction except for causal interpretation.. Fix: The stated theorem environments prove weaker-than-supported claims and obscure the actual frontier of assumptions needed for identification versus inference.

Referee notes: I reproduced the full-class support-completion endpoint, the CE joint-kernel representation, the separated/band comparisons, the LP envelope derivative, and the one-step expansion. The algebraic term lists mostly match, but the headline claims rely on missing setup conditions and ambiguous front-matter definitions.
